data.title: Wall Street's Cautious Approach Amid Economic Data and Fed Rate Concerns
data.description:
According to PANews, Wall Street is exhibiting caution this week as the relief from the end of the historic U.S. government shutdown fades. Concerns are mounting over upcoming economic data and the Federal Reserve's ability to execute a rate cut in December. This risk-averse sentiment has intensified the sell-off of Bitcoin, which has fallen from its record high in early October, showing only slight gains in 2025. The leading digital asset has dropped below the $95,000 mark. CoinGecko data reveals that after a $19 billion liquidation on October 10, which led to a $1 trillion evaporation in the total cryptocurrency market value, the crypto market remains under pressure.

Key events this week include:

- Tuesday 02:00 (UTC+8): 2026 FOMC voting member and Minneapolis Fed President Kashkari hosts a fireside chat.

- Thursday 03:00 (UTC+8): The Federal Reserve releases its monetary policy meeting minutes, followed by a speech from FOMC permanent voting member and New York Fed President Williams.

- Friday 02:40 (UTC+8): 2025 FOMC voting member and Chicago Fed President Goolsbee delivers a speech.

- Friday 05:30 (UTC+8): 2026 FOMC voting member and Philadelphia Fed President Paulson discusses economic outlook.

- Friday 20:30 (UTC+8): FOMC permanent voting member and New York Fed President Williams speaks.

- Friday 22:00 (UTC+8): 2026 FOMC voting member and Dallas Fed President Logan participates in a panel at the "2025 Swiss National Bank and Its Observers" event.

The market has taken note of numerous comments from so-called "inflation hawks." Investors have reduced the likelihood of a December rate cut to below 50%. Prior to the Fed's October meeting, the market had almost fully priced in a December rate cut.

data.title: Bitcoin News: Bitcoin Drops Below $95K as AI Market Crash and ETF Outflows Hit Crypto Sentiment
data.description: Key TakeawaysBitcoin drops to a six-month low as weakness in AI and tech stocks triggers broad risk-off sentiment.Spot Bitcoin ETF outflows and selling from a long-term 2011 wallet added downside pressure.Derivatives show muted demand for bullish leverage, but whales at major exchanges bought the dip.Bitcoin fell to a six-month low of $94,590 on Friday, extending an 11% weekly decline as the broader tech sell-off spilled into crypto markets. The downturn came as AI-related stocks suffered sharp drawdowns, reducing overall risk appetite and limiting demand for leveraged long exposure.The move erased $900 million in BTC leveraged long positions, though this accounted for less than 2% of total open interest — a far smaller impact than the cascading liquidations seen on Oct. 10, when thin liquidity amplified losses across derivatives markets.Macro Fears Add Pressure as Traders Reassess RiskU.S. macro uncertainty added to the bearish mood. President Donald Trump’s renewed push to cut tariffs to reduce food prices raised fresh concerns about upward inflation pressure. Allianz chief economic adviser Mohamed El-Erian warned of rising recession risks as lower-income households face an “affordability crunch” that could spread across the economy.Derivatives Show Weak Bullish Demand But Limited Bear ConvictionDespite the sell-off, the BTC futures premium remained near 4%, slightly below the neutral 5% threshold but above recent lows. The data indicates muted bullish leverage, yet does not signal strong conviction from bears either.AI Sector Crackdown Fuels Sell-Off as ETF Outflows AccelerateThe correction across risk assets was partly driven by a sudden reversal in the once-dominant AI narrative. Concerns around inflated earnings, rising costs, and slowing demand triggered significant drawdowns in leading AI-focused firms.Legendary investor Michael Burry questioned whether companies have been propping up earnings by extending depreciation schedules for computing hardware, noting that Amazon was the only major tech firm to shorten its depreciation timetable.At the same time, U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs saw $1.15 billion in net outflows over two days, adding further pressure. While the amount represents less than 1% of total ETF assets under management, the timing coincided with increased uncertainty.Additional selling emerged from a single 2011-era Bitcoin holder, though analysts emphasized the event was isolated and not indicative of broader long-term investor behavior.Options Market Shows Resilience Despite Price DropThe 30-day BTC options delta skew held near 10%, above the neutral 6% level but well below the 16% fear peak seen last month. Despite Bitcoin being down 24% from its all-time high, the options market has remained relatively steady, suggesting resilience among sophisticated traders.Broader Risk Markets Are Derisking — Keeping Bitcoin Under PressureMultiple companies with market caps above $20 billion — including CoreWeave, Nebius Group, Symbiotic, Super Micro Computer, and Ubiquiti — posted double-digit declines since Nov. 5. With recession fears rising and volatility returning, traders continue shifting toward cash and defensive assets.Unless macro and AI-sector uncertainty eases, Bitcoin may remain under sustained pressure, even if derivatives positioning hints that some pro traders are beginning to buy the dip.

data.title: Solana News: Solana Price Drops to 5-Month Low as ETF Inflows Slow; Analysts Warn of Potential Fall to $100
data.description: Solana’s price extended its sharp decline this week, hitting a five-month low even as spot Solana exchange-traded funds (ETFs) continue to attract steady inflows. Analysts warn that a drop toward the $100 level is becoming increasingly likely after SOL lost multiple key support zones.SOL ETFs Extend Inflows but Momentum WeakensSpot Solana (SOL) ETFs recorded their thirteenth consecutive day of inflows on Thursday, highlighting continued institutional demand for the network’s native token.According to SoSoValue, Solana ETFs added $1.49 million during the session, bringing cumulative inflows to $370 million and total assets above $533 million.The Bitwise Solana ETF (BSOL) was the only product to record inflows on the day, marking the weakest session for Solana ETFs since launching on Oct. 28.The softening momentum reflected broader market stress. Spot Bitcoin ETFs saw $866 million in outflows on Thursday — the second-worst day since launch — while spot Ether ETFs recorded $259.2 million in outflows, reducing cumulative inflows to $13.3 billion.SOL Price Breaks Multi-Year UptrendDespite the persistent ETF demand, Solana’s price action deteriorated rapidly. SOL has dropped more than 34% over the past two weeks, falling to $142 on Friday, its lowest level since June 23.The decline pushed Solana below its 100-week simple moving average (SMA) and broke the multi-year uptrend that began in January 2023. Analysts now view the $95 region — the yearly low — as the next major support.Glassnode data shows the network’s daily order block around $140 offers limited protection. The UTXO realized price distribution (URPD) indicates weak buyer clustering below this level, suggesting few holders are defending the range.If SOL loses the $140 zone decisively, traders expect a move toward the 200-week SMA near $100, widely considered the “last line of defense.”Momentum Indicators Signal Further WeaknessSolana’s relative strength index (RSI) has fallen to its lowest reading since April 2025, reinforcing the bearish trend.As previously reported, a clean break below $150 opened downside targets at $126 and then $100.With ETF inflows slowing, macro pressure rising and key technical supports failing, analysts warn that Solana may face additional selling before a sustainable recovery can form.

data.title: Analysis: Cryptocurrency market capitalization has given back its year-to-date gains, and its correlation with macroeconomic risks may remain high.
data.description: Kaito data shows that Bitcoin market depth has decreased by approximately 30% from its high this year, indicating a significant contraction in market liquidity (Note: Market depth refers to the market's ability to absorb large transactions without significant price fluctuations). In the options market, traders are increasingly betting on volatility, with rising demand for neutral strategies such as straddles and knockouts. Cryptocurrency market capitalization has already given back its year-to-date gains, and market sentiment is likely to remain subdued until further positive news emerges. Max Gokhman, an executive at Franklin Templeton, stated that the correlation between cryptocurrencies and macroeconomic risks will likely remain high until institutions become more deeply involved in the cryptocurrency market and their investments are no longer limited to Bitcoin and Ethereum. (Bloomberg)

data.title: Bitcoin mining company CleanSpark spends $460 million to buy back 30.6 million shares of common stock.
data.description: Nasdaq-listed Bitcoin mining company CleanSpark announced it has repurchased 30.6 million ordinary shares for $460 million. This repurchase did not involve any shares held by the company's directors or executives. The company also announced the completion of its previously disclosed $1.15 billion convertible senior note offering. (PRNewswire)

data.title: Investor Increases Ethereum Holdings Despite Losses
data.description:
According to BlockBeats On-chain Detection, investor Huang Licheng, known as 'Brother Ma Ji,' has recently increased his Ethereum holdings by purchasing an additional 200 ETH. Over the past eight hours, he has accumulated a total of 350 ETH. Currently, his leveraged position of 25 times on Ethereum is experiencing a floating loss of $1.925 million, equating to a 195% loss. His total position now stands at 7,750 ETH, valued at $24.6 million, with an entry price of $3,422 and a liquidation price of $3,047.

Additionally, Huang has placed limit orders to close 475 ETH positions at prices ranging from $3,188 to $3,400.

data.title: Berkshire Hathaway Adjusts Portfolio with Alphabet Investment Amid Apple Stock Reduction
data.description:
According to PANews, Berkshire Hathaway, led by U.S. President Donald Trump, has disclosed a $4.3 billion stake in Alphabet, Google's parent company, while simultaneously reducing its holdings in Apple. This move positions Alphabet as the tenth largest holding in Berkshire's portfolio. The decision marks a departure from Warren Buffett's traditional strategy of investing in value stocks rather than high-growth companies, as he approaches retirement at the end of the year.

In the third quarter, Berkshire Hathaway sold approximately $11 billion worth of Apple shares. Aside from its investments in Apple and Alphabet, Berkshire's largest stock positions do not include major technology companies. Its other significant holdings, which remained largely unchanged during the third quarter, include American Express, Bank of America, and Coca-Cola.

data.title: Founders Fund Reduces Stake in Bitmine
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, recent filings with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission reveal that Founders Fund, led by billionaire and prominent Silicon Valley investor Peter Thiel, has sold half of its shares in Bitmine. The venture capital firm now holds 2,547,001 shares in the company.

Previously, it was reported that Founders Fund Growth II Management disclosed a 9.1% stake in BitMine Immersion Technologies as of July 8.

data.title: Hong Kong SFC Flags HKTWeb3 as Suspicious Virtual Asset Platform
data.description: According to PANews, the Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission (SFC) has added a virtual asset trading platform named 'HKTWeb3' to its list of suspicious virtual asset trading platforms. The SFC's website indicates that HKTWeb3 is not licensed by the commission. Although the platform's website claims a partnership with a licensed virtual asset trading platform, the licensed entity has confirmed that it has no association with HKTWeb3.

data.title: Aster clarified that its token economic model remains unchanged, and future unlocked tokens will be transferred to a separate address.
data.description: Aster, a perpetual contract provider, issued a statement on the X platform clarifying that the recent update to the ASTER token economic model has caused confusion within the community. Aster stated that the ASTER token economic model remains unchanged. The update aimed to accurately reflect the circulating supply of tokens and address issues regarding the monthly unlocking allocation for the community and ecosystem. The monthly unlocked token allocation has remained unused and has not been included in the circulating supply of ASTER tokens. Unlocked tokens will be transferred to a separate public unlocking address, but there are currently no plans or needs to spend funds from this address.

data.title: American Bitcoin, a Bitcoin mining company affiliated with the Trump family, released its Q3 financial report: it added approximately 3,000 BTC during the quarter.
data.description: American Bitcoin, a Bitcoin mining company associated with the Trump family, released its Q3 2025 financial report, disclosing that it increased its holdings by approximately 3,000 BTC during the quarter. As of September 30, 2025, its Bitcoin holdings were approximately 3,418 BTC, and as of November 13, its Bitcoin holdings had increased to 4,090 BTC. The company mined 563 BTC in Q3, and the number of BTC pledged or otherwise secured during the same period was 2,385. Furthermore, the company's Q3 revenue was $64.2 million, net profit was $3.5 million, and total hashrate reached approximately 25.0 EH/s, representing a quarter-over-quarter increase of approximately 2.5 times in mining capacity. (PRNewswire)
